name: Port of Soller and Faro des Cap Gros from Soller
name_original: Port de Sóller y Faro des Cap Gros desde Sóller — circular por el Paraje Natural de la Serra de Tramuntana
description: This difficult 14.5-mile route from Port de Soller to Faro des Cap Gros offers stunning views but is not recommended for touring bikes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Fornalutx are situated in the Serra de Tramuntana mountains of Mallorca, Spain. The region is characterized by rugged rocky peaks, lush green valleys, and a landscape dotted with citrus groves and ancient olive trees. Traditional stone houses and narrow cobbled streets define the village itself, offering a blend of natural and architectural features. The terrain includes both demanding mountain ascents and gentler routes through valleys and along coastal areas. Best touring cycling routes around Fornalutx The most popular touring cycling route is Port of Soller and Faro des Cap Gros from Soller, a 14.5 miles (23.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ascent with rewarding views of the coastline and lighthouse. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sa Capelleta – Monnàber Tunnel loop from Sóller, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.4 km) path. It winds through the scenic Sóller Valley, passing traditional agricultural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Cap Gros Lighthouse – Sóller Old Town loop from Sóller, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the historic town, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Fornalutx is defined by the dramatic Serra de Tramuntana mountains, fragrant citrus groves, and coastal roads with sea views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely valley rides to demanding mountain climbs. The routes in Fornalutx are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 23,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fornalutx's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Fornalutx?
A: There are nearly 50 touring cycling routes around Fornalutx, offering a wide range of experiences from easy valley rides to challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 23,000 times.

Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find in Fornalutx?
A: Fornalutx offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes. You'll find options for all skill levels, including 9 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes. This variety allows cyclists to choose between leisurely rides through citrus groves or demanding climbs in the Serra de Tramuntana mountains.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the Fornalutx area?
A: Yes, Fornalutx offers several family-friendly touring cycling routes, particularly those that wind through the Sóller Valley. These routes often feature gentler gradients and pass through picturesque agricultural landscapes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Oil Monument – Sóller Old Town loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 11.2 km ride.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Fornalutx?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Fornalutx are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Capelleta – Monnàber Tunnel loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the scenic Sóller Valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Gros Lighthouse – Sóller Old Town loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views and a visit to the historic town.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see along the cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Fornalutx pass by numerous natural and historical attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/177273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúber Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1109784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg Blau Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or visit charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biniaraix&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also offer stunning vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/135809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Port de Sóller Bay and Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints on the touring cycling routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The Serra de Tramuntana mountains provide numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Routes like the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003654432/port-of-soller-and-faro-des-cap-gros-from-soller" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Soller and Faro des Cap Gros from Soller&lt;/a&gt; offer rewarding views of the coastline and lighthouse. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/754387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Cúber Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/84094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Sa Rateta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Fornalutx?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Fornalutx is typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for ideal cycling conditions. The landscapes are also particularly vibrant, with blooming flowers in spring and comfortable temperatures in autumn.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Fornalutx?
A: The touring cycling routes around Fornalutx are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic Serra de Tramuntana mountains, the fragrant citrus groves, and the stunning coastal roads with sea views.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous Gorg Blau Aqueduct?
A: Yes, some routes in the region allow you to experience the impressive Gorg Blau Aqueduct. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic lake view – Aqueduct of Sa Calobra loop from Embassament de Cúber&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to ride near this architectural feat while enjoying scenic lake views.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Fornalutx or the surrounding area?
A: While Fornalutx is a small village, public transport options exist in the wider Sóller Valley. The historic Sóller tram connects Sóller to Port de Sóller, and buses serve various towns in the Serra de Tramuntana. It's advisable to check specific bus lines and their policies regarding bicycle transport, as space can be limited, especially during peak season.

Q: Where can I park my car if I'm bringing my bike to Fornalutx?
A: Fornalutx has limited parking within the village itself due to its narrow streets. However, there are usually public parking areas available on the outskirts of the village. For routes starting from Sóller, which is very close, you can find more extensive parking options, often near the town center or the port.

Q: Are there opportunities for cafe stops or places to refuel along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the Fornalutx area pass through charming villages and towns like Sóller, Biniaraix, or even Fornalutx itself. These locations offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and small shops where you can stop for a coffee, a meal, or to refill your water bottles, making it easy to refuel during your ride.
tips: Roadside view to the town, nice for photos.

Very artistically designed chapel, so far little noticed by tourism.
The interior of the chapel was modeled after a cave. Through a unfortunately locked door, the statues of the Blessed Virgin Mary and Saint Sant Bernadette can be seen behind and above the altar.
If you come here in the afternoon, the area around the chapel is a wonderful place of rest.

Since there is parking here, it is a good starting point for hikes. But be careful, the access road from above is very narrow and a bit difficult with oncoming traffic, but is more suitable for the German average petrol car driver than the access from below.
Suddenly you are away from the crowds and you cycle between the orange trees.
Great view of the harbor of Port de Sóller, the Torre Picada and the Puig de Bàlitx.

A really good ice cream parlor opposite the Sóller market hall. However, it's not exactly cheap.

The ice cream is also available in the refrigerated section of some supermarkets; I saw it at Müller in Artà and at Hipercentro in Port de Pollença.

There's also a Fet a Sóller shop attached, offering typical island specialties, but the prices are quite steep these days.

Some of the products are now also available in supermarkets at significantly lower prices.

Lined with numerous bars and cafes, the main square, Plaza Constitución, is Sóller's beating heart and is home to the valley's distinctive landmark, the Church of Sant Bartomeu. The tram that connects the city and the port fights its way across this magnificent square, ringing the bell.

The beautifully renovated modernist building Can Prunera in Sóller houses a beautiful permanent exhibition (including Kandinksy, Picasso, Warhol, as well as local artists Miquel Barceló and Francesca Martí), as well as temporary exhibitions. The Balearic Museum of Natural Sciences (Museu Balear de Ciències Naturals) and the Jardí Botànic, a garden with many different endemic plants, are just a few minutes' walk from the town center on the road towards the port.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: While komoot may categorize this route for touring bicycles, it is generally not recommended for touring cycling. The trail features a challenging mix of asphalt, paved sections, and significant portions of unpaved, natural, and gravel paths, including singletrack. It's better suited for hiking or mountain biking, requiring good fitness and advanced skills for cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Faro des Cap Gros?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging. You'll encounter asphalt and paved sections, but also substantial parts of unpaved, natural, and gravel paths. Some sections are narrow singletrack. Hikers should wear good footwear due to rocky paths, and mountain bikers may need to push their bikes in some areas.

faqs.question: How challenging is the ascent to Faro des Cap Gros?
faqs.answer: The ascent to Cap Gros Lighthouse from Port de Sóller is 1.7 km long with an average gradient of 5.2%. Be prepared for some very steep sections, especially towards the end. It requires good fitness, particularly if you're cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route?
faqs.answer: For this circular route starting from Sóller, you can typically find parking options within Sóller or Port de Sóller. Given the popularity of the area, especially for cyclists, arriving earlier in the day is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 23.3 km long. For a touring bicycle, it's estimated to take around 1 hour and 45 minutes of active riding time. However, given the challenging terrain, especially for touring bikes, and the desire to enjoy the scenery, allow for more time.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking scenery. You'll get panoramic views of Port de Sóller harbor, the Mediterranean Sea, and the high peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains. Key highlights include the Cap Gros Lighthouse, View of Fornalutx, and Sa Capelleta. The route also passes through ancient cultivated lands and olive groves.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park generally allows dogs, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always ensure your dog is capable of handling the varied and sometimes challenging terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potential for heavy rains in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or cycling on public trails within the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park. However, always respect private property and follow local regulations, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that are part of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route, including 'GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Stage 5: Muleta - Tossals verds' and 'GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Stage 4: Can Boi - Muleta'. It also crosses 'El Camí IT4TR4 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera' and 'Port de Sóller - Sóller'.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is more natural, you'll find amenities in Sóller and Port de Sóller at the start and end. Along the way, you might pass by the The Ice Cream Factory (Sóller) for a treat, and the Constitution Square and Sant Bartomeu Church, Sóller offers a place to relax. The Refugi de Muleta, a former military telegraph station, also serves as a hostel for hikers, offering a potential resting point.

waypoints.name: Constitution Square and Sant Bartomeu Church, Sóller
